hook: Presence is often communicated before your words get a fair chance.
body: People read tension, pace, orientation, and eye behavior long before they assess the quality of your banter.

hook: Many men obsess over lines while broadcasting uncertainty through rushed motion and divided attention.
body: Your body should help your message land instead of quietly contradicting it.

hook: Your body should help your message land instead of quietly contradicting it.
body: Standing still for one extra beat, facing the person cleanly, and letting your voice finish can make the same words feel stronger.

hook: Standing still for one extra beat, facing the person cleanly, and letting your voice finish can make the same words feel stronger.
body: Practice slowing your first three seconds in any interaction: feet planted, shoulders open, voice steady.
